rationale: The citing paper uses Netzer and Srinivasan (2011) for a specific interpretive role in the Discussion, not just as a generic literature list item. It argues that complexity-driven shifts in decision weights may explain why adding partial-profile conjoint information to self-explicated data did not improve choice prediction in that prior study. Although the citation is brief and not part of the core model development, it is a concrete empirical touchpoint that supports the paper's implications about conjoint design and predictive transfer.
relation_description: The citing paper leverages a specific finding from the cited paper as corroborative evidence for its interpretation that partial-profile conjoint information may transfer poorly to real-world choice when complexity changes. It uses the cited result to reinforce its broader argument about complexity-induced strategy and weight shifts.
